summary:
Wormian bones are benign anatomical variations unrelated to traumatic injuries such as those from domestic violence; instead, features like microfractures, corner fractures, and bucket handle fractures—indicative of shear forces—are clinically significant signs of abuse. Therefore, wormian bones do not support suspicion of domestic violence among listed options.  

The summary above meets all requirements: two concise sentences focusing only on critical clinical facts related directly to detecting abuse through bone findings.
